purpose: Provide reusable benchmark cases that test whether AOF review can reject weak human-facing artifacts instead of over-scoring them from an AI-internal perspective.

cases.name: Intentionally Weak Artifact Rejection
cases.scenario: Feed a plain but clearly weak proposal/deck/explanation artifact that a human would likely reject.
expected_fail_if: review says approved or solid without concrete blocking criticism
review explains issues only in abstract AI-internal language
expected_pass_if: review marks the artifact as blocked or changes-requested
review explains why a first-time viewer/buyer would reject it

cases.name: Human-Low AI-High Conflict
cases.scenario: Take an artifact that a human reviewer has already judged low quality and verify that AOF records the conflict as a benchmark failure if AI still over-scores it.
expected_fail_if: human-low/ai-high is treated as acceptable disagreement
the artifact remains green after human disconfirmation
expected_pass_if: the conflict is recorded as benchmark failure
the artifact is reopened or routed for stronger review

cases.name: Before/After Quality Delta
cases.scenario: Compare an initial artifact and an improved version and require the reviewer to explain why the latter is better in human-facing terms.
expected_fail_if: review cannot point to concrete quality delta
review says improved without audience-facing explanation
expected_pass_if: review links improvements to role/loop changes when applicable

cases.name: Multi-Loop Improvement Trace
cases.scenario: Require the reviewer to explain what changed across at least three loops and whether the earlier miss was corrected.
expected_fail_if: success is claimed from a single pass
the reviewer cannot explain which critique improved the result
expected_pass_if: the trace explains which loop introduced the key correction
the reviewer can identify previous blind spots
